untranslatable_reason: This signal is a theoretical pricing framework for valuing call options under dividend scenarios, not a price-action trading rule. It requires: (1) option valuation model inputs (strike price, time to expiration, dividend yield, dividend dates) which are not available in the allowed indicator set; (2) comparison of dividend discount intensities across multiple future dividend dates; (3) volatility adjustments conditional on stock volatility levels. The signal describes a mathematical adjustment to theoretical option pricing, not a tradeable entry/exit condition based on technical indicators or price data.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_7 is sourced from an options valuation chapter and describes how to adjust the present value of an underlying stock when pricing call options that span dividend payment dates. This requires access to: dividend amounts, ex-dividend dates, option expiration date, stock volatility classification, and risk-free rate—none of which can be derived from OHLCV price action or the allowed technical indicators. The signal cannot be operationalized as a backtest rule without a custom options pricing engine.
